2606.10216.title: A Source Domain is All You Need: Source-Only Cross-OS Transfer Learning for APT Anomaly Detection via Semantic Alignment and Optimal Transport
2606.10216.paper_summary: Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs) are stealthy, multi-stage cyberattacks whose detection is difficult due to scarce labeled traces, severe class imbalance, and the challenge of generating realistic malicious behavior. These challenges are amplified in cross-operating-system (cross-OS) settings, where a detector trained on one source platform must be deployed on an unlabeled target platform without access to target-domain labels. We study this source-only cross-OS APT detection problem using system-level provenance traces and propose a transport-based framework for ranking anomalous target processes under zero target supervision. The framework abstracts process behavior into structured natural-language descriptions, embeds them using pretrained language models, and constructs a source-normal reference for target scoring. It combines three evidence channels: semantic deviation from source-normal prototypes, structural deviation captured by graph autoencoding, and geometric deviation measured through Optimal Transport (OT). The main contribution is an OT-based barycentric anomaly score that projects target embeddings onto the source-normal manifold and quantifies residual transport mismatch. We further introduce entropy-weighted, angle-aware, and density-aware OT variants to capture uncertainty, directional drift, and sparse-support behavior. Evaluation on DARPA Transparent Computing data spanning Linux, Windows, BSD, and Android, across two APT scenarios and twelve cross-OS transfer pairs, shows that the proposed framework improves ROC-AUC and nDCG over source-only anomaly-detection baselines. The results demonstrate that source-only provenance modeling, combined with semantic abstraction and OT-based anomaly scoring, can support practical cross-platform APT detection without target-domain supervision.
2606.10216.paper_summary_zh: 進階持續威脅（APTs）是隱蔽的多階段網路攻擊，由於標記痕跡稀少、類別不平衡嚴重以及生成現實惡意行為的挑戰，使得其檢測變得困難。這些挑戰在跨作業系統（cross-OS）環境中更為明顯，在這種情況下，訓練於一個源平台的檢測器必須在沒有目標領域標籤的情況下部署到一個未標記的目標平台。我們使用系統級來源追蹤研究這一僅源的跨作業系統 APT 檢測問題，並提出了一個基於運輸的框架，用於在零目標監督下對異常目標進程進行排名。該框架將進程行為抽象為結構化的自然語言描述，使用預訓練的語言模型進行嵌入，並構建一個源正常參考以進行目標評分。它結合了三個證據通道：來自源正常原型的語義偏差、通過圖自編碼捕獲的結構偏差，以及通過最優運輸（OT）測量的幾何偏差。主要貢獻是一個基於 OT 的重心異常分數，將目標嵌入投影到源正常流形上，並量化殘餘運輸不匹配。我們進一步引入了熵加權、角度感知和密度感知的 OT 變體，以捕捉不確定性、方向漂移和稀疏支持行為。在涵蓋 Linux、Windows、BSD 和 Android 的 DARPA 透明計算數據上，針對兩個 APT 場景和十二個跨作業系統轉移對的評估顯示，所提出的框架在 ROC-AUC 和 nDCG 上優於僅源的異常檢測基準。結果表明，僅源的來源建模結合語義抽象和基於 OT 的異常評分，可以支持實用的跨平台 APT 檢測，而無需目標領域的監督。
2606.10216.author: Sidahmed Benabderrahmanea et.al.
2606.10216.authors: Sidahmed Benabderrahmanea, Petko Valtchev, James Cheney, Talal Rahwan
